questions.question: What is the primary function of unfolding a computational graph in Recurrent Neural Networks?
options: A) To increase the capacity of the network
B) To improve the speed of training
C) To facilitate the computation of gradients
D) To reduce the memory requirements
questions.explanation: Unfolding a computational graph in Recurrent Neural Networks allows for the computation of gradients with respect to the entire sequence, rather than just the last hidden state.

questions.question: Which of the following types of RNNs uses the same weights for both forward and backward passes?
options: A) Simple RNN
B) Bidirectional RNN
C) Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)
D) Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU)
questions.explanation: Bidirectional RNNs use the same weights for both forward and backward passes, allowing the network to capture information from both directions of the input sequence.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of Deep Recurrent Networks over traditional RNNs?
options: A) Ability to capture long-range dependencies
B) Increased capacity for learning complex patterns
C) Improved speed of training
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Deep Recurrent Networks can learn to capture long-range dependencies in the input data, allowing them to model more complex patterns than traditional RNNs.

questions.question: What is the main difference between Recursive Neural Networks and Recurrent Neural Networks?
options: A) The use of recursive functions
B) The use of memory cells
C) The use of bidirectional processing
D) The use of convolutional layers
questions.explanation: Recursive Neural Networks use recursive functions to process the input data, whereas Recurrent Neural Networks use a feedback connection to process the input sequence.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) cell in a Recurrent Neural Network?
options: A) To reset the internal state of the network
B) To selectively forget past inputs
C) To selectively remember past inputs
D) To gate the flow of information
questions.explanation: LSTM cells allow the network to selectively remember past inputs, by controlling the flow of information through the feedback connections.
